description_long: This is a World War II-era fighter airplane with a dark green camouflage paint scheme and red roundels on its wings and fuselage. The aircraft features a single propeller engine at the nose, with visible exhaust flames suggesting it is in operation or under stress. The wings are straight and low-mounted, with rounded tips and no visible winglets. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer, forming a conventional tail design. The cockpit is positioned forward on the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with a clear canopy. The landing gear is fixed in a taildragger configuration, with two main wheels under the wings and a smaller tail wheel.
description: A WWII-era fighter airplane with green camouflage, red roundels, a single propeller engine, and a conventional tail. The cockpit is forward and enclosed, and the landing gear is fixed in a taildragger layout.
description_view_0: Front view showing the propeller, nose, and symmetrical low-mounted wings with visible landing gear.
description_view_1: Oblique top-left view highlighting the cockpit canopy, wing roundels, and visible engine flames.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, roundels, and the straight, low-mounted wing configuration.
description_view_3: Oblique top-right view showing the tail section, fuselage markings, and engine flames.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with rounded tips and no winglets; no visible dihedral or anhedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single-seat canopy.
